## Background

Dr. Sarah Chen brings 15 years at the intersection of gerontechnology and urban planning. Former Director of the MIT AgeLab's Transportation Initiative, where she led the landmark "Mobility & Dignity" study across 30 US cities. Ph.D. in Human-Computer Interaction from Stanford, with doctoral research on voice interface design for cognitively diverse populations.

## Relevant Experience

- **MIT AgeLab (2016-2024):** Led $4.2M research program on autonomous vehicle acceptance among seniors. Published 23 peer-reviewed papers on age-inclusive transportation design.
- **Lyft Accessibility (2014-2016):** Senior Product Manager for Lyft's accessibility features. Shipped wheelchair-accessible vehicle matching and hearing-impaired driver communication tools.
- **WHO Consultant (2022-present):** Advisor on the World Health Organization's "Age-Friendly Cities" transportation guidelines.

## Skills & Expertise

- Voice interface design and natural language processing
- Gerontechnology product development
- Healthcare system integration and HIPAA compliance
- Public-private partnership negotiation
- Autonomous vehicle policy and regulation
- Fundraising: secured $18M across previous ventures and research grants

## Why This Founder

Sarah's unique combination of deep academic expertise in senior mobility, hands-on product experience at a major ride-sharing platform, and established relationships with healthcare systems and regulators positions her to build the trust-first mobility platform that this market requires. Her published research provides both credibility with institutional partners and genuine insight into user needs that competitors building from general-purpose platforms cannot match.
